# UC-16.2.22 · PagerDuty On-Call Schedule Gap — Zero Coverage Window

## Description

Coverage gaps route pages to void and extend MTTA.

## Value

Detects schedule holes before a holiday weekend.

## Implementation

Hourly synthetic audit pull; join schedules to teams lookup.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure: PagerDuty Audit API / Terraform plan logs to Splunk.
• Data: PagerDuty schedule audit or maintenance webhooks (`pagerduty:schedule`, `pagerduty:audit`).
• For directory layout and inputs, see docs/implementation-guide.md.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Hourly synthetic audit pull; join schedules to teams lookup.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following search (then save as report or alert; tune time range and threshold):

```spl
index=alerts sourcetype="pagerduty:schedule" OR sourcetype="pagerduty:audit"
| search "gap" OR "no_coverage" OR "unassigned"
| stats latest(_time) as t by schedule_name, team
| sort -t
```

Understanding this search
Coverage gaps route pages to void and extend MTTA.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the PagerDuty incident timeline and service view for the same window. Reconcile a few incident numbers, state changes, and acknowledgement times. If anything disagrees, check export lag, time zone, and which API fields we map.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Suggested view: Calendar heatmap, Table (schedule × gap).

## SPL

```spl
index=alerts sourcetype="pagerduty:schedule" OR sourcetype="pagerduty:audit"
| search "gap" OR "no_coverage" OR "unassigned"
| stats latest(_time) as t by schedule_name, team
| sort -t
```

## Visualization

Calendar heatmap, Table (schedule × gap).
